title: elaphoglossum dumrongii
intro: Elaphoglossum dumrongii, also known as the Thai fern, is a species of epiphytic fern native to Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum dumrongii are said to include the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to act as an antioxidant and anti-cancer agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared for consumption by brewing the leaves in hot water, and its herbal preparations include infusions, decoctions, and sometimes even topical applications.
